 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-2bn","title":"EPIC: Onboarding TUI","description":"# Epic: Onboarding TUI

## Overview
Build the interactive `onboard` command that teaches users Linux basics and the ACFS workflow through hands-on missions.

## Background & Reasoning
Documentation fails beginners. Interactive learning succeeds. The onboard TUI:
- Teaches by doing, not reading
- Builds muscle memory for key commands
- Introduces tools progressively
- Can be re-run anytime as a refresher

## 7 Missions

### Mission 0: Welcome
- What you now have
- The VPS as playground (safe to break, just re-run ACFS)
- Overview of installed tools

### Mission 1: Linux Navigation
- pwd, ls, cd, mkdir, rm
- /home/ubuntu vs /data/projects
- Navigation shortcuts (dev, proj, p aliases)

### Mission 2: SSH & Persistence
- What SSH is
- Why tmux matters (connection drops, tmux lives)
- Basic attach/detach

### Mission 3: tmux Basics
- Create/attach/detach sessions
- Split panes
- Copy mode basics

### Mission 4: Agent Commands
- cc (Claude), cod (Codex), gmi (Gemini)
- First login flow
- `caam backup` right after login

### Mission 5: NTM Command Center
- ntm deps -v (verify)
- ntm tutorial
- ntm spawn demo --cc=2 --cod=1
- ntm send/broadcast

### Mission 6: NTM Command Palette
- Show vendored command_palette.md
- Searchable prompt list
- "Send to all agents" feature

### Mission 7: The Flywheel Loop
- Plan in Beads → bv
- Coordinate via Agent Mail
- Run agents via NTM
- Search via cass
- Build memory via cm
- Scan with ubs
- Optional guardrails via slb

## Success Criteria
- [ ] All 7 missions implemented
- [ ] Can be re-run safely anytime
- [ ] Works offline (except optional updates)
- [ ] Takes < 30 minutes to complete all
- [ ] User feels confident to start coding

## Technical Approach
- Shell script or simple Go/Rust binary
- Uses gum or similar for TUI elements
- Stores progress in ~/.acfs/onboard_progress.json","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:21:25.187518-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T12:21:20.499516-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T12:21:20.499516-05:00","close_reason":"All sub-tasks completed: 2bn.1 (onboard TUI shell script). Interactive menu with gum support, progress tracking, and completion markers implemented.","labels":["epic","onboard"]}
 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-2bn.1","title":"Create onboard TUI shell script","description":"# Task: Create Onboard TUI Shell Script

## Description
Build the interactive onboarding TUI that teaches users the ACFS workflow.

## Location
Install to: `~/.local/bin/onboard`

## UX Flow

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│           Welcome to ACFS Onboarding                │
│                                                     │
│  Choose a lesson:                                   │
│                                                     │
│  [1] Welcome & Overview                ✓ completed  │
│  [2] Linux Navigation (5 min)          ● in progress│
│  [3] SSH & Persistence                              │
│  [4] tmux Basics                                    │
│  [5] Agent Commands (cc, cod, gmi)                  │
│  [6] NTM Command Center                             │
│  [7] NTM Prompt Palette                             │
│  [8] The Flywheel Loop                              │
│                                                     │
│  [q] Quit  [r] Restart from beginning               │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

## Implementation Options

### Option A: Pure Bash + gum
Use charmbracelet/gum for TUI elements:
```bash
gum choose "Welcome" "Linux Navigation" "SSH & Persistence" ...
```

### Option B: Simple menu
Plain bash with read:
```bash
while true; do
    clear
    echo "ACFS Onboarding"
    echo "1) Welcome"
    echo "2) Linux Navigation"
    ...
    read -p "Choose: " choice
done
```

## Lesson Format
Each lesson:
1. Brief explanation
2. Command to try
3. Expected output
4. Checkpoint/quiz

## Progress Tracking
Store in ~/.acfs/onboard_progress.json:
```json
{
  "completed": [1, 2, 3],
  "current": 4,
  "started_at": "2025-12-19T..."
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All 8 lessons implemented
- [ ] Progress saved between runs
- [ ] Can be re-run anytime
- [ ] Works over SSH
- [ ] Clear and beginner-friendly","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:26:56.967996-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T12:17:32.895359-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T12:17:32.895359-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented interactive onboard TUI with: gum-based menu when available, fallback simple menu, progress tracking in ~/.acfs/onboard_progress.json, lesson completion markers, --reset flag, updated 1-8 numbering. Commit d96659e.","labels":["onboard","task"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-2bn.1","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-2bn","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:26:56.969656-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-2du","title":"Set up bun workspaces for monorepo","description":"# Task: Set up Bun Workspaces

## Description
Configure bun workspaces in the root package.json to manage:
- apps/web (Next.js wizard website)
- packages/manifest (manifest utilities)
- packages/onboard (onboard TUI)

## Technical Details
Root package.json should include:
```json
{
  "workspaces": ["apps/*", "packages/*"]
}
```

Each workspace needs its own package.json with appropriate name/scripts.

## Reasoning
Bun workspaces allow:
- Shared dependencies across packages
- Single lockfile (bun.lock)
- Easy cross-package imports
- Consistent tooling

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Root package.json with workspaces config
- [ ] Each workspace has valid package.json
- [ ] `bun install` works from root
- [ ] Cross-package imports resolve correctly

## Dependencies
Depends on directory structure being created first.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:20:33.61383-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T23:27:33.126119-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-19T23:27:33.126119-05:00","close_reason":"Root bun workspace set up with apps/* and packages/* workspaces","labels":["infrastructure","task"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-2du","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-e88","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:20:33.616805-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-5m6","title":"EPIC: VPS Installer (install.sh)","description":"# Epic: VPS Installer (install.sh)

## Overview
Build the one-liner bash installer that transforms a fresh Ubuntu VPS into a fully-armed agentic coding environment.

## Background & Reasoning
This is the "magic moment" - the user pastes one command and everything happens. The installer must:
- Work when run as root (common VPS default)
- Work when run as ubuntu with sudo
- Be idempotent (safe to run twice)
- Handle connection drops and resume
- Log everything for debugging

## Design Philosophy
**Vibe Mode Default**: Passwordless sudo, agents with full permissions, minimal friction.
**Checkpointed Phases**: Each phase writes state so reruns skip completed work.
**Module Manifest**: Single source of truth (acfs.manifest.yaml) drives all installs.

## Installer Phases
1. Preflight + confirm OS (Ubuntu 24.04+ / 25.x)
2. Create/normalize `ubuntu` user + sudoers
3. Base packages (apt)
4. Shell (zsh + oh-my-zsh + p10k + plugins)
5. Dev toolchain (bun, uv, rust, go, tmux, rg, ast-grep)
6. Agent stack (claude/codex/gemini CLIs + aliases)
7. Flywheel stack (ntm, slb, ubs, cass, cm, bv, caam, mcp_agent_mail)
8. Post-install UX (onboard + doctor + summary)

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Fresh Ubuntu 25.x VPS → working environment in < 15 minutes
- [ ] Safe to run twice without breaking anything
- [ ] All 8 Dicklesworthstone tools installed and verified
- [ ] `acfs doctor` shows all green
- [ ] Logs available at /var/log/acfs/install.log

## Considerations
- Always prefer upstream installers when available
- Verify each tool after install
- Provide clear error messages with fix suggestions
- Support `--dry-run` for transparency","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:21:06.097272-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T12:01:44.001564-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T12:01:44.001564-05:00","close_reason":"All 12 sub-tasks completed. VPS installer fully modularized with libraries for: logging, OS detection, user normalization, shell setup, CLI tools, language runtimes, coding agents, Dicklesworthstone stack, cloud/database tools, smoke test, and canonical zshrc.","labels":["epic","installer"]}
 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-5m6.1","title":"Create installer logging library (scripts/lib/logging.sh)","description":"# Task: Create Installer Logging Library

## Description
Build a reusable logging library for consistent installer output.

## Functions

```bash
log_step()    # [1/8] Step description (blue)
log_detail()  # Indented detail (gray)
log_success() # ✔ Success message (green)
log_warn()    # ⚠️ Warning message (yellow)
log_error()   # ✖ Error message (red)
log_debug()   # Only if ACFS_DEBUG=1
```

## Requirements
- All output to stderr (keep stdout clean)
- Support --quiet flag (suppress non-errors)
- Support --verbose flag (show debug)
- Timestamps in log file
- ANSI color codes with fallback

## Log File
Write all output to:
- `/var/log/acfs/install.log`
- `~/.acfs/logs/install.<timestamp>.log`

## Implementation

```bash
#!/bin/bash
# scripts/lib/logging.sh

ACFS_LOG_FILE="/var/log/acfs/install.log"
ACFS_QUIET="${ACFS_QUIET:-0}"
ACFS_VERBOSE="${ACFS_VERBOSE:-0}"

log_step() {
    local step="$1"
    local msg="$2"
    echo -e "\033[34m[$step] $msg\033[0m" >&2
    echo "[$(date -Iseconds)] STEP: $msg" >> "$ACFS_LOG_FILE"
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All log functions implemented
- [ ] Colors work in terminal
- [ ] --quiet suppresses output
- [ ] --verbose shows debug
- [ ] Log file written with timestamps
- [ ] Works when sourced by other scripts","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:24:17.871116-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T23:23:59.34494-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-19T23:23:59.34494-05:00","close_reason":"Files created in previous session: scripts/lib/logging.sh, os_detect.sh, user.sh, zsh.sh, and install.sh with all 8 phases","labels":["installer","task"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-5m6.1","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-5m6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:24:17.872789-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
